



The main idea of this assignment is to have the students read in xlsx data, skip 10 rows, and extract information using regex.

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as pd
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```


```{python}
data = pl.read_csv('StarWarsOriginal.csv')
data.columns




```

```{python}
rename_dict={
    "Have you seen any of the 6 films in the Star Wars franchise?" : "seen_any",
    "Do you consider yourself to be a fan of the Star Wars film franchise?" : "star_wars_fan",
    "Which of the following Star Wars films have you seen? Please select all that apply." : "seen_episode_I",
    "Unnamed: 4" : "seen_episode_II",
    "Unnamed: 5" : "seen_episode_III",
    "Unnamed: 6" : "seen_episode_IV",
    "Unnamed: 7" : "seen_episode_V",
    "Unnamed: 8" : "seen_episode_VI",
    "Please rank the Star Wars films in order of preference with 1 being your favorite film in the franchise and 6 being your least favorite film." : "rank_episode_I",
    "Unnamed: 10" : "rank_episode_II",
    "Unnamed: 11" : "rank_episode_III",
    "Unnamed: 12" : "rank_episode_IV",
    "Unnamed: 13" : "rank_episode_V", 
    "Unnamed: 14" : "rank_episode_VI",
    "Please state whether you view the following characters favorably, unfavorably, or are unfamiliar with him/her." : "han_solo",
    "Unnamed: 16" : "luke_skywalker",
    "Unnamed: 17" : "princess_leia_organa",
    "Unnamed: 18" : "anakin_skywalker",
    "Unnamed: 19" : "obi_wan_kenobi",
    "Unnamed: 20" : "emperor_palpatine",
    "Unnamed: 21" : "darth_vader",
    "Unnamed: 22" : "lando_calrissian",
    "Unnamed: 23" : "boba_fett",
    "Unnamed: 24" : "C-3P0",
    "Unnamed: 25" : "R2_D2",
    "Unnamed: 26" : "jar_jar_binks",
    "Unnamed: 27" : "padme_amidala",
    "Unnamed: 28" : "yoda",
    "Which character shot first?" : "who_shot_first",
    "Are you familiar with the Expanded Universe?" : "know_expanded_universe",
    "Do you consider yourself to be a fan of the Expanded Universe?æ": "expanded_universe_fan",
    "Do you consider yourself to be a fan of the Star Trek franchise?" : "star_trek_fan",
    "Gender" : "gender",
    "Age" : "age",
    "Household Income" : "household_income",
    "Education" : "education",
    "Location (Census Region)" : "location"

}

rename_dict = {
    # Seen / fan questions
    "Have you seen any of the 6 films in the Star Wars franchise?": "seen_any",
    "Do you consider yourself to be a fan of the Star Wars film franchise?": "star_wars_fan",

    # Seen episodes (multi-select)
    "Which of the following Star Wars films have you seen? Please select all that apply.": "seen_episode_I",
    "": "seen_episode_II",               # actual blank header
    "_duplicated_0": "seen_episode_III",
    "_duplicated_1": "seen_episode_IV",
    "_duplicated_2": "seen_episode_V",
    "_duplicated_3": "seen_episode_VI",
    # There should be one more for episode VI
    # but your printed header list DOES NOT include Unnamed: 8 → episode VI
    # it seems Polars removed an empty column and shifted things
    # Check the data or confirm manually.
    # For now I'll assume the next logical one:
    "_duplicated_4": "rank_episode_II",  # but see below

    # Ranking episodes
    "Please rank the Star Wars films in order of preference with 1 being your favorite film in the franchise and 6 being your least favorite film.": "rank_episode_I",
    "_duplicated_4": "rank_episode_II",
    "_duplicated_5": "rank_episode_III",
    "_duplicated_6": "rank_episode_IV",
    "_duplicated_7": "rank_episode_V",
    "_duplicated_8": "rank_episode_VI",

    # Character favorability
    "Please state whether you view the following characters favorably, unfavorably, or are unfamiliar with him/her.": "han_solo",
    "_duplicated_9": "luke_skywalker",
    "_duplicated_10": "princess_leia_organa",
    "_duplicated_11": "anakin_skywalker",
    "_duplicated_12": "obi_wan_kenobi",
    "_duplicated_13": "emperor_palpatine",
    "_duplicated_14": "darth_vader",
    "_duplicated_15": "lando_calrissian",
    "_duplicated_16": "boba_fett",
    "_duplicated_17": "C_3PO",
    "_duplicated_18": "R2_D2",
    "_duplicated_19": "jar_jar_binks",
    "_duplicated_20": "padme_amidala",
    "_duplicated_21": "yoda",

    # General questions
    "Which character shot first?": "who_shot_first",
    "Are you familiar with the Expanded Universe?": "know_expanded_universe",
    "Do you consider yourself to be a fan of the Expanded Universe?��": "expanded_universe_fan",
    "Do you consider yourself to be a fan of the Star Trek franchise?": "star_trek_fan",

    # Demographics
    "Gender": "gender",
    "Age": "age",
    "Household Income": "household_income",
    "Education": "education",
    "Location (Census Region)": "location"
}
```

```{python}
# Read the file with the real header, then skip the next two junk rows
data = pl.read_csv(
    "StarWarsOriginal.csv",
    skip_rows_after_header=1
)

# Now apply your renaming dictionary
data = data.rename(rename_dict)

data.head()
```

Now that the columns are fixed in polars, I need to generate emails for them to extract names form

```{python}
import random

# set seed so I can get the results again
random.seed(42)

# sample of first names
first_names = [
    # modern common
    "Liam", "Noah", "Oliver", "Elijah", "James", "William", "Benjamin", "Lucas", "Henry", "Alexander",
    "Emma", "Olivia", "Ava", "Sophia", "Isabella", "Charlotte", "Amelia", "Mia", "Harper", "Evelyn",

    # modern extras
    "Mason", "Logan", "Levi", "Sebastian", "Jack", "Owen", "Aiden", "Samuel", "Daniel", "Jacob",
    "Emily", "Grace", "Ella", "Chloe", "Aria", "Lily", "Nora", "Audrey", "Lucy", "Hannah",

    # FANCY / NOBLE / LATIN-FLAVOR
    "Maximus", "Reginald", "Octavius", "Cassius", "Atticus",
    "Evander", "Lysander", "Valerius", "Percival", "Alistair",
    "Seraphina", "Ophelia", "Valentina", "Celestia", "Arabella",
    "Evangeline", "Octavia", "Genevieve", "Isolde", "Marcella",

    # heroic / fantasy-ish
    "Gideon", "Thaddeus", "Magnus", "Ronan", "Dorian",
    "Benedict", "Callum", "Soren", "Leander", "Theron",
    "Elowen", "Rowena", "Brielle", "Felicity", "Rosalind",

    # more contemporary variety
    "Caleb", "Ryan", "Nathan", "Christian", "Isaiah",
    "Julian", "Thomas", "Wesley", "Miles", "Asher",
    "Scarlett", "Madison", "Elena", "Maya", "Violet",
    "Stella", "Caroline", "Ruby", "Alice", "Josephine",

    # a few rare but not too weird
    "Marcellus", "Lucian", "Cyrus", "Aurelia", "Helena",
    "Tobias", "Damien", "Silas", "Phoebe", "Lavinia"
]

last_names = [
    # common U.S.
    "Smith", "Johnson", "Williams", "Brown", "Jones", "Garcia", "Miller", "Davis", "Rodriguez", "Martinez",
    "Hernandez", "Lopez", "Gonzalez", "Wilson", "Anderson", "Thomas", "Taylor", "Moore", "Jackson", "Martin",
    "Lee", "Perez", "Thompson", "White", "Harris", "Sanchez", "Clark", "Ramirez", "Lewis", "Robinson",

    # traditional European
    "Bennett", "Fletcher", "Crawford", "Hawkins", "Montgomery", "Barrett", "Lawrence", "Hudson", "Hayes", "Reynolds",
    "Richards", "Armstrong", "Douglas", "Chambers", "Preston", "Wallace", "Barlow", "Dalton", "Sawyer", "Winston",

    # noble / aristocratic / fancy
    "Kingsley", "Harrington", "Ashford", "Wellington", "Beauregard",
    "Kensington", "Blackwood", "Ravenscroft", "Stormwind", "Winterborne",
    "Holloway", "Everhart", "Redmond", "Langford", "Haverhill",

    # mythic / heroic / fantasy-ish
    "Stormrider", "Ironwood", "Shadowbane", "Brightshield", "Wolfhart",
    "Starcrest", "Frosthelm", "Silverwind", "Nightwell", "Thornfield",

    # old-world / classy
    "Devereaux", "Fontaine", "Lachance", "Bellerose", "Dumont",
    "Kavanagh", "OMalley", "Fitzroy", "Ludwick", "Weiss",
    "Kovacs", "Hoffmann", "Schneider", "Ricci", "Moretti",

    # rare but elegant
    "Carrington", "Ashdown", "Marwood", "Bramwell", "Loxley",
    "Kingsford", "Aldridge", "Pennington", "Somerfield", "Hartswell"
]


# make unique 1200 pairs
pairs = set()
while len(pairs) < 1200:
    fn = random.choice(first_names)
    ln = random.choice(last_names)
    pairs.add((fn, ln))

# convert to polars DataFrame
name_df = pl.DataFrame(
    list(pairs),
    schema=["first_name", "last_name"]
)

name_df
```

Make the emails for each name


```{python}
# Set seed for reproducibility
np.random.seed(42)

# probabilities
# gmail: 60%, org: 25%, yahoo: 15%
probs = [0.6, 0.25, 0.15]
domains = ["gmail", "myorg", "yahoo"]

# create a random choice column
rand_vals = np.random.rand(name_df.height)


def assign_email(val):
    if val < probs[0]:
        domain = "gmail"
    elif val < probs[0] + probs[1]:
        domain = "myorg"
    else:
        domain = "yahoo"
    
    # Random capitalization
    case_type = np.random.choice(["upper", "lower", "random"])
    if case_type == "upper":
        domain = domain.upper()
    elif case_type == "lower":
        domain = domain.lower()
    else:  # random
        domain = "".join([c.upper() if np.random.rand() > 0.5 else c.lower() for c in domain])
    
    # Random spaces
    # if np.random.rand() < 0.2:
    #     domain = " " + domain
    if np.random.rand() < 0.2:
        domain = domain + " "
    
    return domain

# apply to Polars DataFrame
email_col = [assign_email(v) for v in rand_vals]

name_df = name_df.with_columns(
    pl.Series("email_type", email_col)
)

name_df.head(10)
```


Now I need to create their actual gmail column

```{python}
# def make_email(first, last, domain):
#     domain_clean = domain.strip().lower()  # remove spaces and lowercase
#     if "gmail" in domain_clean:
#         return f"{first.lower()}.{last.lower()}@gmail.com"
#     elif "yahoo" in domain_clean:
#         return f"{last.lower()}_{first.lower()}@yahoo.com"
#     elif "myorg" in domain_clean:
#         random_numbers = "".join([str(np.random.randint(0, 10)) for _ in range(5)])
#         return f"{last[:5].lower()}{first[:3].lower()}{random_numbers}@myorg.com"
#     else:
#         return None

# # Apply using Polars `apply` on multiple columns
# name_df = name_df.with_columns(
#     pl.struct(["first_name", "last_name", "email_type"]).apply(
#         lambda s: make_email(s["first_name"], s["last_name"], s["email_type"])
#     ).alias("email")
# )

# name_df.head(10)
```

```{python}
STARTNUM = 54541

# number of rows
n_rows = name_df.height

# random starting number between 0 and 90000
start_num = STARTNUM

# create sequential numbers starting from start_num, zero-padded 5 digits
seq_nums = (pl.arange(0, n_rows, eager=True) + start_num).cast(pl.Utf8).str.zfill(5)

# add the column to the dataframe
name_df = name_df.with_columns([
    seq_nums.alias("unique_num")
])

# now build the email column
name_df = name_df.with_columns([
    pl.when(pl.col("email_type").str.to_lowercase().str.contains("gmail"))
      .then(
          pl.concat_str([
              pl.col("first_name").str.to_lowercase(),
              pl.lit("."),
              pl.col("last_name").str.to_lowercase(),
              pl.lit("@") + pl.col("email_type")  # preserve jank
          ])
      )
    .when(pl.col("email_type").str.to_lowercase().str.contains("yahoo"))
      .then(
          pl.concat_str([
              pl.col("last_name").str.to_lowercase(),
              pl.lit("_"),
              pl.col("first_name").str.to_lowercase(),
              pl.lit("@") + pl.col("email_type")
          ])
      )
    # .otherwise(
    #     pl.concat_str([
    #         pl.col("last_name").str.slice(0,5).str.to_lowercase(),
    #         pl.col("first_name").str.slice(0,3).str.to_lowercase(),
    #         pl.col("unique_num"),  # unique per row
    #         pl.lit("@") + pl.col("email_type")
    #     ])
    # )
    .otherwise(
        pl.concat_str([
            pl.col("last_name").str.to_lowercase(),
            pl.col("unique_num"),  # unique per row
            pl.col("first_name").str.to_lowercase(),
            pl.lit("@") + pl.col("email_type")
        ])
    )
    .alias("email")
])

name_df.head(10)

```



Make an email for every respondentID 
```{python}
print(data.shape)
print(name_df.shape)

# trim name_df to match the number of rows in data
n_rows = data.height  # 1186
name_df = name_df.head(n_rows)

# add the email column to data
# assuming name_df has an "email" column
data = data.with_columns(name_df["email"].alias("email"))

# check
print(data.shape)  # should now be (1186, original_columns + 1)
# data.select(["RespondentID", "email"]).head(10)
data.head(3)
```

then shuffle the name_df before saving it so you can't just match rows

```{python}
# shuffle all rows
name_df_shuffle = name_df.sort(pl.col('email'), descending=True)
name_df_shuffle.head()
```




```{python}
cols = data.columns

# move 'email' right after 'RespondentID'
cols.remove("email")
new_order = ["RespondentID", "email"] + [c for c in cols if c != "RespondentID"]

# reorder
data = data.select(new_order)

data.head(3)
```

Finally I need to add ten nonsense rows above the header so they have to use skip.



```{python}
import polars as pl
import os

# Use current directory or Windows temp
temp_path = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "starwars.csv2")
# or: temp_path = r"C:\Users\mecha\starwars.csv"

# write the normal CSV first (with header)
data.write_csv(temp_path)

# Now prepend 10 nonsense lines
with open(temp_path, "r") as f:
    original = f.read()

# create 10 nonsense lines
nonsense = "\n".join(["This,is,a,nonsense,line"] * 10) + "\n"

# overwrite the file with nonsense + original CSV
with open(temp_path, "w") as f:
    f.write(nonsense + original)

print(f"CSV written with nonsense lines at {temp_path}")

```


```{python}
# import polars as pl

# data_raw = pl.read_csv(
#     temp_path,
#     try_parse_dates=False,
#     dtypes=pl.Utf8,        # force all columns to strings
#     ignore_errors=True      # skip problematic rows
# )

# data_raw.head(15)

```


```{python}
data_skipped = pl.read_csv(temp_path, skip_rows=10)
data_skipped.head(3)

```


```{python}
import polars as pl

# path to save locally
local_path = "starwars_skip_rows102.csv"

# write the current data (with email) to CSV
data.write_csv(local_path)

# Now prepend 10 nonsense rows above the header
with open(local_path, "r") as f:
    original = f.read()

# create 10 nonsense lines
nonsense = "\n".join(["This,is,a,nonsense,line"] * 10) + "\n"

# overwrite the file with nonsense + original CSV
with open(local_path, "w") as f:
    f.write(nonsense + original)

print(f"CSV saved locally as {local_path} with 10 nonsense rows prepended.")
```


```{python}
name_df.write_csv("names2.csv")
```
